objective: Generate a repository-specific 3B public-first adapter for cases that passed public replay eligibility screening.
constraints: Do not compare ABI, raw pointer values, memory ownership, or raw return types when languages differ.
Derive observable behavior from source tests, fixtures, expected files, and assertion intent.
Use only public target APIs for L1 replay.
Every behavior case in source_evidence.behavior_cases already passed structural public replay screening.
Required target substitutions for mixed public/internal cases must be called explicitly in the Rust harness.
Adapter generation should convert eligible cases into replay events; generation failures remain unresolved_adapter_generation rather than semantic exclusions.
The LLM generates a replay hypothesis; correctness is decided only by compiling and running target replay.

generation_policy.expected_behavior_rule: Every executable assertion must be grounded in source test evidence, fixtures, or a direct public API property implied by that evidence. Use expected_behavior_confidence=high only when the source test/fixture gives a concrete expected result.
generation_policy.coverage_rule: Cover every eligible source behavior case listed in source_evidence.behavior_cases when it can be converted into a reliable Rust replay. Cases that cannot be converted by the adapter generator are reported as unresolved_adapter_generation; behavior differences should be replayed rather than hidden as exclusions.

public_eligible_pairs_with_src_test_evid.src_signature: CJSON_PUBLIC(cJSON *) cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive(const cJSON * const object, const char * const string)
CJSON_PUBLIC(cJSON_bool) cJSON_Compare(const cJSON * const a, const cJSON * const b, const cJSON_bool case_sensitive)

notes: Source is treated as C-style function API for 3B inventory and adapter synthesis.

summary.purpose: Compact source-test evidence for adapter synthesis. behavior_cases carry expected-behavior evidence; function_index maps every source-tested public 3A source function to concrete case ids.
summary.behavior_case_selection_policy: Only cases that passed exact-alignment and structural public-replay eligibility screening are adapter conversion obligations. Mixed cases include required target public substitutions.

field_guide.behavior_cases: Primary evidence for operation/expected-behavior design. Each case has concrete source assertions, literals, and aligned source function UUIDs. Full snippets are intentionally omitted from the initial context to keep all eligible cases visible; targeted agent passes receive snippets for the specific missing cases they are asked to cover. Mixed cases expose non_public_aligned_call_names so adapter generation can avoid dropping explicit internal state transitions that affect final observable behavior.
field_guide.function_index: Coverage index keyed by source UUID. case_refs point back to behavior_cases.case_id; direct_assertions are only a quick per-function hint, not a replacement for behavior_cases.
field_guide.fixtures: Input or expected-output files referenced by source tests when available.
field_guide.mixed_public_internal_cases: All public L1 candidate cases that also explicitly call non-public aligned functions. These are risk cases: use them for L1 only when the adapter can preserve the internal effect through target public behavior; otherwise leave them unresolved for L1 replay.

target_project_context.cargo_toml:
[package]
name = "json"
version = "0.12.4"
authors = ["Maciej Hirsz <hello@maciej.codes>"]
description = "JSON implementation in Rust"
repository = "https://github.com/maciejhirsz/json-rust"
documentation = "https://docs.rs/json/"
license = "MIT/Apache-2.0"
edition = "2018"
target_project_context.note: Use Cargo.toml package/lib names and src/lib.rs public exports when writing Rust integration tests. This context intentionally omits most docs/comments and keeps public surface declarations.

target_crate_import_hint.integration_test_note: In tests/cp2rs_3b_public.rs, import the target crate as `json` unless Cargo.toml/lib.rs evidence shows a different public crate name.

rust_integration_test_contract.crate_import_rule: Target public items must be called as `json::item` or imported from `json`.
rust_integration_test_contract.fragment_merge_rule: Adapter case generation merges each rust_test_body independently into one integration test file. Do not rely on imports emitted by a different case fragment.
rust_integration_test_contract.import_scope_rule: A Rust `use` inside one #[test] function is scoped only to that function. Put shared imports at file top level, or include imports inside every test body that uses them, or use fully qualified crate paths.
rust_integration_test_contract.repair_rule: Replay repair may fix imports, Rust syntax, borrow/API-call shape, and integration-test build issues, but must not change source-test-derived expected behavior.

target_api_scope.selection_policy: target_public_api_signatures is a synthesis-focused candidate list: all 3A aligned target APIs plus public APIs from nearby owners/files and common support names. Validation still checks against the full target public API set parsed from the repository.

target_public_api_signatures.signature: pub fn pretty(&self, spaces: u16) -> String

pub fn push<T>(&mut self, value: T) -> Result<()>
    where T: Into<JsonValue>

pub fn insert<T>(&mut self, key: &str, value: T) -> Result<()>
    where T: Into<JsonValue>
pub fn array_remove(&mut self, index: usize) -> JsonValue
pub fn remove(&mut self, key: &str) -> JsonValue
fn index_mut(&mut self, index: usize) -> &mut JsonValue
pub fn as_fixed_point_i64(&self, point: u16) -> Option<i64>
pub fn as_fixed_point_u64(&self, point: u16) -> Option<u64>
pub fn contains<T>(&self, item: T) -> bool where T: PartialEq<JsonValue>
pub fn to_writer<W: Write>(&self, writer: &mut W)
fn index_mut(&mut self, index: String) -> &mut JsonValue
pub fn write<W: Write>(&self, writer: &mut W) -> io::Result<()>
pub fn write_pretty<W: Write>(&self, writer: &mut W, spaces: u16) -> io::Result<()>
pub fn new(writer: &'a mut W, spaces: u16) -> Self
pub fn new(writer: &'a mut W) -> Self
pub fn from<T>(value: T) -> JsonValue where T: Into<JsonValue>
pub fn new(f: u64, e: isize) -> Self
pub fn as_fixed_point_i64(&self, point: u16) -> Option<i64>
pub fn as_fixed_point_u64(&self, point: u16) -> Option<u64>
pub fn from_parts(positive: bool, mut mantissa: u64, mut exponent: i16) -> Self
pub unsafe fn from_parts_unchecked(positive: bool, mantissa: u64, exponent: i16) -> Self
fn from_iter<I: IntoIterator<Item=(K, V)>>(iter: I) -> Self
pub fn get_mut(&mut self, key: &str) -> Option<&mut JsonValue>
pub fn insert(&mut self, key: &str, value: JsonValue)
pub fn remove(&mut self, key: &str) -> Option<JsonValue>
pub unsafe fn from_f64(d: f64) -> Self

required_adapter_shape.name: llm_synthesized_<src>_to_<tgt>_public_v1

operation_name.description: Behavior scenario derived from source tests.
target_functions: target uuid from 3A or support public target uuid
operation_name.normalization: Observable behavior comparison rule grounded in source test evidence.

source_case_ids: required: explicit case_id values from source_evidence.behavior_cases covered by this replay; events without source_case_ids do not count toward behavior-case coverage
trace_events.case_grouping_rationale: required only when source_case_ids contains more than one case: explain why their normalized inputs and expected observable behavior are equivalent

excluded_behavior_cases.details: required: concrete source/target evidence explaining why public replay is not possible

required_adapter_shape.rust_test_harness: Complete Rust integration test source for tests/cp2rs_3b_public.rs. It must define exactly one #[test] fn for each trace_events[].id, using the id as the function name.
